ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for cranberry white chocolate chip cookies; FOODON_03315091 (cookie) is the closest defensible broader class.
ontology.foodex2_description: Biscuits and cookies — sweet baked goods category
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000E covers sweet biscuits/cookies as a category; no specific cranberry white chocolate chip cookie code exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard retail pack of cranberry white chocolate chip cookies weighing 200g, equivalent to approximately 8–10 medium-sized cookies depending on individual cookie weight.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cookies, chocolate chip, commercially prepared (SR Legacy ID 18158) and Cookies with fruit filling (adapted composite)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input per_100g values (energy 450 kcal, protein 5g, fat 24g, carbohydrate 55g, sugar 32.5g, sodium 200mg, dietary fibre 2g, saturated fat 14g, trans fat 0.5g, cholesterol 50mg, calcium 50mg, iron 1mg, vitamin C 0.5mg) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for commercially prepared chocolate chip cookies and white chocolate chip cookies with dried fruit. Values are retained from input as they fall within the plausible range for this product category. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for commercially prepared chocolate chip cookies (FDC ID 18158) reports energy ~488 kcal, protein 5.4g, fat 23.5g, carbohydrate 63.5g, sodium 348mg — the input values reflect a slightly lower-energy, lower-sodium formulation consistent with a white chocolate and cranberry variant. Adapted composite used; values accepted as reasonable for this product type.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Cookie / sweet biscuit analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cranberry white chocolate chip cookies in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 59 is estimated from the closest analogous food: 'Cookies, plain/sweet biscuit' (GI ~55–65, median 59) as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values. The high sugar content (32.5g/100g) and refined flour base support a medium GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (59 × 110g carbohydrate in 200g serving) / 100 = 59 × 1.10 = 64.9. Serving carbohydrate = 55g/100g × 200g = 110g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (32.5g/100g) and saturated fat (14g/100g) promoting insulin-driven sodium retention and arterial stiffness; sodium at 200mg/100g is moderate but secondary to metabolic effects
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit cranberry white chocolate chip cookies due to the high sugar load (32.5g/100g) which drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain, and the high saturated fat content (14g/100g) which contributes to arterial stiffness — both independently elevating blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines, regardless of the moderate sodium level.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very high sugar (32.5g/100g) far exceeding the 20g/100g glycaemic ceiling, high refined carbohydrate load (55g/100g) with minimal fibre (2g/100g), and medium GI (~59) producing a very high glycemic load of 64.9 per 200g serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid cranberry white chocolate chip cookies; the sugar content of 32.5g/100g far exceeds safe glycaemic thresholds, the refined carbohydrate load of 55g/100g with only 2g/100g dietary fibre will cause severe postprandial glucose spikes, and a single 200g pack delivers a glycemic load of approximately 65 — well beyond the recommended daily GL ceiling for diabetes management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 14g/100g (70% of daily limit per 100g) and trans fat at 0.5g/100g from butter and white chocolate chips, combined with high sugar promoting dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid cranberry white chocolate chip cookies due to the very high saturated fat content (14g/100g, representing approximately 70% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit per 100g serving), the presence of trans fat (0.5g/100g), and the high sugar load (32.5g/100g) which collectively promote LDL elevation, endothelial dysfunction, and systemic inflammation — all established CVD risk factors per AHA/ACC 2019 guidelines.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 14g/100g triggering TNF-alpha and IL-6 pro-inflammatory cytokine pathways; sugar 32.5g/100g further promoting systemic inflammation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid cranberry white chocolate chip cookies as the saturated fat content (14g/100g, nearly three times the 5g/100g pro-inflammatory threshold) from butter and white chocolate chips strongly activates TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways, and the high sugar load (32.5g/100g) further amplifies inflammatory mediator production — avoid entirely during flares and limit strictly during remission.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cranberry White Chocolate Chip Cookies are commercially baked sweet cookies made from a refined wheat flour dough enriched with butter, white sugar, brown sugar, and eggs, studded with creamy white chocolate chips and pieces of dried sweetened cranberries. The cookies are baked until golden with a soft, chewy interior and lightly crisp edges. White chocolate chips contribute a rich, sweet, milky flavour with no cocoa bitterness, while dried cranberries add a tart-sweet contrast and a slightly chewy texture. The product is sold in a 200g retail pack, typically containing 8–10 medium-sized cookies. Nutritionally, these cookies are energy-dense at 450 kcal per 100g, with a high fat content (24g/100g, of which 14g is saturated fat from butter and white chocolate), very high sugar (32.5g/100g), and a moderate protein content (5g/100g) from eggs and flour. Dietary fibre is low at 2g/100g. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 59 (medium GI) based on analogous sweet biscuit data, but the glycemic load per 200g serving is very high at approximately 65, making this product clinically unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The trans fat content (0.5g/100g) and high saturated fat further elevate cardiovascular risk. This product is vegetarian but not vegan, contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, and may contain soy from white chocolate chip emulsifiers. It is widely available in Singapore bakeries, cafes, and grab-and-go food platforms as a snack or dessert item.

confidence_reason: Input nutrient values from GrabFood (AI-generated, confidence 0.7) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for commercially prepared chocolate chip cookies with dried fruit and white chocolate. Values have been cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy entry 18158 and found to be within the plausible range for this product category. Confidence is set to medium because no exact product-specific laboratory analysis from an approved primary source was available; values are adapted from the closest USDA FoodData Central equivalent. GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature. Cultural metadata (primary_cuisine: Donut, ethnic_tags: Coffee/Donut/Bagel) from the input was incorrect and has been corrected to Western/International Bakery.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific cranberry white chocolate chip cookie formulation is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18158 or the Singapore HPB database as of 2026-04-16. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific product formulation not available in approved sources consulted. 2. Input cultural metadata was erroneous (primary_cuisine: 'Donut', ethnic_tags: ['Coffee', 'Donut', 'Bagel']) and has been corrected to 'Western / International Bakery' with appropriate tags. 3. Input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) does not correspond to the HPB Nutrient Composition database and was not used as a nutrient evidence source; USDA FoodData Central was used as the approved source. 4. Glycemic index of 59 is an analogous estimate based on sweet biscuit/cookie data from Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables of GI and GL Values (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for cranberry white chocolate chip cookies exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 5. Trans fat value of 0.5g/100g is retained from input; this is plausible for commercially baked cookies using partially hydrogenated fats or butter, though many modern formulations have reduced trans fat. Verification against product label is recommended. 6. The 200g portion size is confirmed from the input as the standard retail pack size. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; white chocolate chips may contain non-halal emulsifiers — marked is_halal: false pending verification.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected erroneous cultural metadata (primary_cuisine from 'Donut' to 'Western / International Bakery'; ethnic_tags corrected from ['Coffee','Donut','Bagel'] to appropriate bakery context).
Replaced forbidden/unsupported source (foodo.sg URL not matching HPB nutrient database) with USDA FoodData Central as approved nutrient source.

Added glycemic_load_per_serving (64.9) calculated from GI × carbohydrate per 200g serving.
Corrected health_context to use required three conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes with correct human_condition_name, Cardiovascular Disease) with clinical guidance.
Documented all -1 fields in notes per g3 requirement.
